package main

import (
	"log"
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/driver/postgres"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Positive scenario for migration_runner_and_api_server_bootstrap_coupled.
// Rule intent: One startup path that always runs migrations and serves traffic together.
// Anti-pattern: RunMigrations() and StartServer() coupled in same bootstrap function.

type Order struct {
	ID       uint    `gorm:"primaryKey"`
	Total    float64 `gorm:"column:total"`
	Status   string  `gorm:"column:status"`
}

func bootstrapAndStart(dsn string) {
	db, err := gorm.Open(postgres.Open(dsn), &gorm.Config{})
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("failed to connect database: %v", err)
	}

	// Migration runner coupled with server bootstrap
	if err := db.AutoMigrate(&Order{}); err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("migration failed: %v", err)
	}

	r := gin.Default()
	r.GET("/orders", func(c *gin.Context) {
		var orders []Order
		db.Find(&orders)
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, orders)
	})

	log.Fatal(r.Run(":8080"))
}

func main() {
	bootstrapAndStart("postgres://localhost:5432/mydb")
}
